diagrams.title: Hash Function Process Flow
diagrams.description: Shows how input message is processed through hash function to produce fixed-size output
elements: Padding (to make multiple of block size)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding hash function structure and fixed output property

diagrams.title: HMAC Algorithm Structure
diagrams.description: Illustrates nested hash construction in HMAC with inner and outer keys
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how HMAC uses double hash with key padding for security

diagrams.title: Message Authentication with MAC
diagrams.description: Shows sender and receiver process for authenticating messages using shared secret key
elements: Sender: Message → MAC Function (with Key) → MAC Tag
Receiver: Extract Message → MAC Function (same Key) → Compare MAC Tag
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how MAC provides integrity and authentication

diagrams.title: Birthday Attack Probability
diagrams.description: Graph showing probability of collision vs number of samples relative to hash space size
elements: X-axis: Number of inputs (as fraction of 2^n)
Y-axis: Probability of at least one collision
Curve shows sharp increase around sqrt(2^n)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing why birthday attack reduces security from 2^n to 2^(n/2)

diagrams.title: Digital Signature Process
diagrams.description: Shows asymmetric approach using private key for signing and public key for verification
elements: Sender: Message → Hash → Sign (with Private Key) → Signature
Receiver: Hash Message → Verify (with Sender's Public Key) → Compare Hashes
Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) for public key distribution
